title: Understand: First Amendment: Religion—Overview
description: Check your understanding of the First Amendment with this set of multiple-choice questions.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Establishment Clause of the First Amendment primarily addresses which issue?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Freedom of speech
Government involvement in religion
The right to keep and bear arms
The right to a fair trial

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which scenario would likely violate the Establishment Clause of the First Amendment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A government permitting parents to use state scholarship funds to pay for tuition at religious schools
A city using a government grant to refurbish playgrounds at both religious and non-religious daycare facilities
A state law allowing public schools to have their students recite a short, voluntary prayer at the beginning of each school day
A town council meeting that opens its meetings with a prayer from local clergy from all faiths

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these is a violation of the Free Exercise Clause?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A church requiring members to attend weekly services
A government agency denying unemployment benefits to public employees who were fired for using an illegal drug in a religious ceremony
An executive order that limits attendance at religious services but not at businesses during a pandemic
The government spending tax money to finance a war that someone opposes on religious grounds

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Free Exercise Clause of the First Amendment protects an individual’s right to do what?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Criticize the government
Practice their chosen religion
Keep and bear arms
Petition the government for redress of grievances

assessment_items.question_content:
**In which situation would the Free Exercise Clause be *most* relevant?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A parent challenging a state scholarship program for excluding religious schools
A city council passing a law restricting freedom of speech
A state university limiting student protests on campus
A police department implementing new dress code regulations for officers

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the words or phrases that best complete the sentence.**

The two clauses central to the protection of the freedom of religion in the First Amendment are the \_\_\_\_ Clause and the \_\_\_\_ Clause.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Free Worship; Restricted Exercise
Prayer; Necessary and Proper
Establishment, Free Exercise
Supremacy; Church and State

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which colonies did NOT have established churches at the time of America’s founding? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Rhode Island
Pennsylvania
Georgia
Virginia

assessment_items.question_content:
**By what year had all states ended official status for established churches?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What is true about the freedom of conscience?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It can be regulated only by local governments.
It’s an unalienable right because people have a right and a duty to think for themselves.
It’s an alienable right that people may have to give up to ensure national security.
It has nothing to do with the First Amendment.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement about the First Amendment at the time of the nation’s founding is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It prohibited only Congress, not state or local governments, from establishing or disestablishing churches.
It applied to every level of government, as the First Amendment was immediately incorporated.
It set limits on state and local governments, but not on the national government.
It applied only to Christianity, as there was very little religious diversity across the states.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement about religious practices in the states at the founding is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Almost every state had a law identifying religions citizens could not practice, including Catholicism and Judaism.
Almost every state recognized the Church of England as the national church and forbade the practice of other religions.
Almost every state constitution included an establishment clause forbidding the state from establishing a religion.
Almost every state constitution included a free exercise clause, which protected people’s right to worship freely.

assessment_items.question_content:
**When is it lawful for the government to interfere with an individual’s right to practice a religion?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: When the religious doctrine conflicts with federal or state laws
When the religion requests funds from practitioners during services
When the religion does not use a holy book as the foundation for its doctrine
When the religious practices violate the rights or safety of others
